headline: Norwegian Cruise Line falls on weak guidance despite Q1 beat
summary: Investing.com -- Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NCLH) reported first-quarter results that exceeded earnings expectations but fell short on revenue, while lowering its full-year outlook due to geopolitical headwinds and softer demand, sending shares down 6.3% premarket.

content.title: Norwegian Cruise Line falls on weak guidance despite Q1 beat
content.summary: Investing.com -- Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NYSE:NCLH) reported first-quarter results that exceeded earnings expectations but fell short on revenue, while lowering its full-year outlook due to geopolitical headwinds and softer demand, sending shares down 6.3% premarket.

headline: Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) Misses Q1 CY2026 Sales Expectations
summary: Cruise company Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) fell short of the market’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, but sales rose 9.6% year on year to $2.33 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.23 per share was 61.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) Misses Q1 CY2026 Sales Expectations
content.summary: Cruise company Norwegian Cruise Line (NYSE:NCLH) fell short of the market’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, but sales rose 9.6% year on year to $2.33 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $0.23 per share was 61.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Reports First Quarter 2026 Financial Results
summary: MIAMI, May 04, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings Ltd. (NYSE: NCLH) (together with NCL Corporation Ltd. (“NCLC”), “Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings”, “Norwegian”, “NCLH” or the “Company”) today reported financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 and provided guidance for the second quarter and full year 2026. Highlights First quarter total revenue grew 10% to $2.3 billion. GAAP net income was $105 million, with EPS of $0.23.Delivered Adjusted EBITDA1 of $533

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: eBay, GameStop, FedEx, UPS, Norwegian, Micron, and More
summary: Stocks were mostly higher Monday after the U.S. military’s Central Command denied a report that Iran had struck an American warship in the Strait of Hormuz.  Shares of  eBay  climbed 5.6% to $109.92 after  GameStop  disclosed it was making a $56 billion bid to buy the e-commerce company.
